title: Preserve Support, Not Correspondence: Dynamic Routing for Offline Reinforcement Learning

method.name: DROL (Dynamic Routing for Offline Reinforcement Learning)
method.type: one-step actor approach for offline RL
method.key_innovation: Dynamic routing where dataset actions are routed to the nearest candidate
method.process: For each state, samples K candidate actions and routes each dataset action to its nearest candidate using k*(s,a) = arg min_{k∈[K]} ||â^k - a||₂². Only the winner gets updated with behavior cloning and Q-improvement.
method.advantage: Allows different latent regions to specialize to different parts of local support while preserving one-step inference

results.performance: Competitive with the one-step FQL baseline, improving many OGBench task groups while remaining strong on both AntMaze and Adroit

problem_addressed: Offline RL requires staying within action regions supported by a fixed dataset, which becomes harder on multimodal benchmarks like OGBench where nearby states can admit several distinct yet reasonable actions

comparison.advantage_over_iterative_methods: Avoids backpropagating through a long iterative sampler and keeps execution cheap
comparison.difference_from_FQL: Unlike FQL-style extraction where ownership stays attached to the same sampled output, in DROL, ownership can transfer across candidates
